name: Twitch API (Helix)
what_it_does: Twitch's Helix API for accessing live stream data, VODs, channel information, subscriptions, and real-time events via EventSub webhooks.
use_cases: Monitoring live streams and detecting when streamers go live
Retrieving viewer counts, game categories, and stream metadata
Building Discord bots or dashboards for Twitch channel status
Automating channel point rewards and chat interactions
Aggregating Twitch clip and VOD data for analysis
not_for: Non-gaming streaming (audience and content is gaming-focused)
Commercial live streaming for business presentations
best_when: You need to integrate with Twitch's gaming/streaming ecosystem or build tools for Twitch streamers and communities.
avoid_when: You need general video hosting, non-gaming live streaming, or fine-grained video analytics.

auth.notes: App access tokens for server-to-server requests. User access tokens (OAuth) for user-context actions. Client ID always required in headers alongside token.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free to use with rate limits. No paid tiers.
pricing.notes: Completely free to use. Developer program requires registration. Commercial products may need review.

performance.rate_limits: 800 points/minute per Client-ID (each endpoint has a cost)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors include status, error type, and message. Pagination cursor errors are sometimes confusing.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency support. Write operations like sending whispers or creating clips are not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Both Client-ID and Authorization headers are required for every request - missing one gives misleading 401 errors
App access tokens must be generated via client_credentials flow and refreshed after expiry
EventSub webhooks require a publicly reachable HTTPS endpoint with signature verification
The legacy Kraken v5 API is deprecated - Helix endpoints differ significantly
Rate limit points vary by endpoint type - read the cost for each endpoint you use
